subject: Persistent TLS handshake failure to https://funwithscience.net/* from scheduled-task sandbox; hostname-specific (other GitHub Pages domains succeed)
summary: From the dome-social scheduled-task sandbox, curl to https://funwithscience.net/* fails with 'SSL routines: unexpected eof while reading' (OpenSSL 0A000126) on 3 of the last 4 calendar days (Apr 28 fail / Apr 29 fail / Apr 30 OK / May 1 fail). Same sandbox same run: raw.githubusercontent.com / john09289.github.io / api.github.com all succeed (HTTP 200). Failure is funwithscience.net-hostname-specific. Operator-side browser access + GSC sitemap fetches confirm the live site is healthy for actual users — this is a sandbox/edge-cycling artifact, not a content-side issue.

observed_pattern.2026-04-28: fail (TLS decode error 562, all 4 GH Pages anycast IPs, all sub-paths)

diagnostic_questions: Is the sandbox's OpenSSL/cipher list compatible with funwithscience.net's specific TLS/Cloudflare/GitHub-Pages-edge configuration?
Does funwithscience.net use a different anycast IP pool or edge cert chain than john09289.github.io (both are GitHub Pages but funwithscience.net is custom-domain via org-level CNAME)?
Is the failure correlated with a specific GitHub Pages edge POP that this sandbox routes to?
Is the intermittency related to the org-level CNAME inheritance (funwithscience-org/funwithscience-org.github.io) introducing a different cert/SNI path?
fallback_already_in_place: dome-social falls back to raw.githubusercontent.com for content verification when funwithscience.net is unreachable. This is sufficient for daily content checks; the only thing we lose is live-smoke-test of the deployed site from the sandbox.
social_self_caveat: Filing this proposal is at the boundary of yesterday's escalation criterion. The criterion was '3rd consecutive day = file' which Apr 30 broke. The contrarian addendum 'if recurrence within 7 days, 3-of-N is worth filing' is what I'm acting on, but '3-of-N pattern' isn't a strict statistical threshold. Operator should feel free to mark this proposal NOT_ACTIONABLE if it's overzealous; the cost of filing-too-early is low (one JSON file).
routing: tinker — environmental probe. NOT a content-side issue. NOT a build-pipeline issue.
recommended_disposition: On next tinker pass, run: (a) `openssl s_client -connect funwithscience.net:443` from sandbox vs same against john09289.github.io, compare TLS handshake details; (b) check sandbox's libcurl/OpenSSL versions; (c) check whether the failure correlates with specific times-of-day. If structural mismatch found, document it; if intermittent, mark as known sandbox quirk and close.
operator_disposition: Closed per dome-social's own recommended_disposition fallback path: "if intermittent, mark as known sandbox quirk and close." Reasoning: user_impact=ZERO, fallback to raw.githubusercontent.com already in place and working, the intermittency pattern (3-of-4 days fail, 1 day ok) is consistent with sandbox-edge-cycling rather than infrastructure-side-issue, no signal value from running openssl s_client probe given zero user-side consequences. dome-social's own self-caveat acknowledged the filing was at-the-boundary of escalation criterion. If TLS failures spread to other GitHub Pages domains used by the pipeline (john09289.github.io, raw.githubusercontent.com, api.github.com) — which would indicate a real sandbox infrastructure issue — file a fresh PROP with the broader pattern. As of close-time none of those failures has been observed. PROP-060 Mode 0 scan (added today) doesn't pick this up because PROP files live in proposals/ not in routing fields; separately, PROPs awaiting operator review are operator-triage territory, not tinker-action.
